title: No support for Jews fleeing Nazi Germany at Evian conference
summary: In July 1938, a conference was held in Evian, France, to address the issue of Jews being persecuted by the Nazis in Germany and Austria. Almost nobody in the world was willing to take them in as refugees.

title: Germany news: Coalition has 'found its footing,' Merz says
summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz says changes to health and labor laws show that his coalition is making progress. Meanwhile, arrests were made after police carry out drug raids in western Germany and the Netherlands.

title: Germany news: Military conscientious objections spike
summary: The number of people applying for conscientious objection to military service has risen sharply and there were around 5,100 heat-related deaths recorded in June alone.

title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes appeal to AfD voters
summary: Germany's chancellor has insisted that his coalition has "regained its footing" — despite dismal poll numbers. Friedrich Merz also cautioned against voting for the far-right AFD party.

title: 150 years of the Bayreuth Festival: Confronting a troubled legacy
summary: Should the past be confronted — or avoided? Why the Wagner festival's handling of its Nazi-era legacy remains a contentious issue in its 150th anniversary year.

title: Running dry: How to store more groundwater for dry seasons
summary: More frequent weather extremes are leading to water shortages during droughts and flooding during heavy rain. But groundwater storage can help secure water for dry seasons while mitigating extreme rain.

title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
summary: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

title: Art biennial Manifesta revives Germany's postwar churches
summary: Following the destruction of World War II, around 1,000 new churches were built in Germany's Ruhr region. Today, many stand empty, but the contemporary art show Manifesta is giving them a new lease on life.

title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?
summary: A quarter of German youth spend too much time on social media, with 350,000 considered to have an addiction problem. Education Minister Karin Prien is advocating for a legal minimum age of 13.
